items.prompt: How does this chapter distinguish data cleaning from preprocessing?
choices: Cleaning corrects errors; preprocessing transforms cleaned values for algorithms
items.explain: Cleaning addresses missing, duplicate, and inconsistent values; preprocessing scales, encodes, and engineers features.

choices: Missing targets can bias supervised training
items.explain: Blank labels force drop or invented labels, both risky for classifiers.

items.prompt: Dropping unused high-cardinality ID columns can improve efficiency without changing the predictive schema.
items.explain: Example 5.2 removes identifiers that never enter the model.

items.prompt: Name two of the four recurring motives for cleaning/preprocessing from this clip.
items.rubric: Any two of: accuracy, comparable scales, efficiency, trustworthy joins.
items.sample_answer: Accuracy on held-out data and computational efficiency.
